arguments.task: Open official Fanbasis pages and determine whether Fanbasis offers a public API or developer platform. Read the homepage, footer, help center, legal pages, pricing pages, and search the site for API, developers, docs, authentication, integrations, and pricing. Also use web search in-browser to find official Fanbasis pages about API, developers, auth, or pricing if linked from official domains. Return only canonical official URLs you actually opened and a concise evidence summary for: product description, auth methods documented for any API, access/pricing gating, API surface type, rate limits, and whether an MCP/server exists.

input.thought: Start browser-based review of official Fanbasis sources and official linked documentation.

raw_result.description: Creator monetization platform for selling memberships, digital products, courses, and fan experiences.
raw_result.auth_notes: No official Fanbasis developer or API authentication documentation was found in the gathered official pages. The reviewed homepage and visible site navigation did not expose API or developer-auth details.
raw_result.gating_reason: The official website presents Fanbasis as a self-serve creator platform with public marketing pages and onboarding flows; no evidence was found of API-specific sales or partner gating.

api_surface.breadth: No public first-party API or developer documentation was found in the reviewed official Fanbasis pages.
api_surface.mcp_notes: No first-party MCP/server documentation was found in the gathered official sources.

raw_result.blocker: No usable public API or developer documentation was found in the official Fanbasis sources reviewed.
raw_result.rate_limit_notes: No public API documentation was found, so no rate-limit information was available.
raw_result.notable_signals: Browser review found homepage/footer links such as About, Features, Help, Support, Privacy Policy, and Terms of Service, but no visible API or Developers link. Attempted pricing page path returned a 404 during review.
raw_result.human_review_reason: Structured fields auth_methods, api_surface.type, and buildability_verdict are inferred from absence of official developer/API documentation in the gathered evidence, and one cited official URL returned a 404 during review, leaving the evidence base thin.
